title: Add(): fix handling of relative paths with no ContextDir
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind bug

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

`Add()` is supposed to handle relative paths when the `ContextDir` value passed to it is not set, but it hasn't been doing that correctly since it was overhauled.  Correct it to recognize relative paths at the function start, when `ContextDir` is not set, and combining them with the current directory to convert them to absolute paths.

#### How to verify it

Added an integration test which should cover the reported cases.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

Fixes #2619.

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

I expect to backport this to the `release-1.16` branch once it passes.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```
The regression in `buildah add` which caused it to not properly handle source locations specified using relative paths should be fixed.
```

comments.body:
I think this is close, but it needs a bit of tweaking perhaps?

```
# pwd
/root
# ls /root/test.txt
/root/test.txt

# buildah add alt-working-container-1 root/test.txt /tmp
error adding content to container "alt-working-container-1": error checking on source /root/root/test.txt under "/": copier: stat: "/root/root/test.txt": no such file or directory
```

> I think this is close, but it needs a bit of tweaking perhaps?
> 
> ```
> # pwd
> /root
> # ls /root/test.txt
> /root/test.txt
> 
> # buildah add alt-working-container-1 root/test.txt /tmp
> error adding content to container "alt-working-container-1": error checking on source /root/root/test.txt under "/": copier: stat: "/root/root/test.txt": no such file or directory
> ```

That looks like what I'd expect.  The current working directory is `/root`, so relative to that, `root/test.txt` would be `/root/root/test.txt`.  What am I missing?
Maybe it was a mis-expectation, but I was expecting it to just add the slash.  @Obirvalger  WDYT?

And @nalind, you're right, maybe I was overthinking.  If I cd to `/`, things work well.

```
# cd /

# buildah add alt-working-container-1 root/test.txt /tmp
15c0b14099637b0c52d1d3ebafd653ca4abe4b8bc4aefccd20351c9b18ea295c
```

Still would like to hear from @Obirvalger
This PR solves the problem. Work with relative paths is even better than just fixing an ambiguous error message.
